ead.archdesc:
======= RMM02984Garnsey family papers2984Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
January 20042B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
agric wwiThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:33:04 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Garnsey family papers
Garnsey family.
Lamb, Elizabeth Pettis. (Title of work: Addition to the Garnsey family history..)
2984
1.9 cubic feet. 1.9 cubic feet.
1847-1993. Diaries, a scrapbook, correspondence, estate and financial papers, a family history, research materials, and other papers. Collection includes diaries, 1850-71; scrapbook about Cyrus Garnsey III and World War I, compiled by his father, Cyrus Garnsey Jr.; letters to Cyrus Garnsey and Mercy Harris Garnsey from Ira Harris, 1847-70; personal letters, clippings, and telegrams, 1917-51, collected by Cyrus Garnsey Jr. about his work with the United States Fuel Administration; estate and financial papers relating to Cyrus Garnsey Jr., Mary Woolsey Pettis and other Garnsey family members, primarily concerning Lakeholme, the Garnsey family home in Seneca Falls, N.Y.; letters, notes, clippings, and research materials about the Sullivan Expedition, historical markers, the Johnston Farm, the Montezuma Marsh bird refuge, Luke Swetland, Judge William I.Grubb, and other interests of Cyrus Garnsey Jr.; also a family history by Elizabeth Pettis Lamb, 1984; and letters relating to the Iroquois turtle shell rattle from Chief Jesse Lyons, Chief of the Onondagas. Maps and letters relating to Cayuga Lake Park; early material relating to Cyrus Garnsey's attending Brown University and his obituaries; Croix de Guerre and memorial certificate for Cyrus Garnsey III, who died in World War I; and letters and pictures relating to Cyrus Garnsey, Jr. and his resemblance to Robert E. Lee. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 ======= 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2067717
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, January 2004
Date modified:
Jude Corina, August 2013
Cite As:
Garnsey family papers, #2984. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Collection includes diaries, 1850-71; scrapbook about Cyrus Garnsey III and World War I, compiled by his father, Cyrus Garnsey Jr.; letters to Cyrus Garnsey and Mercy Harris Garnsey from Ira Harris, 1847-70; personal letters, clippings, and telegrams, 1917-51, collected by Cyrus Garnsey Jr. about his work with the United States Fuel Administration; estate andfinancial papers relating to Cyrus Garnsey Jr., Mary Woolsey Pettis and other Garnsey family members, primarily concerning Lakeholme, the Garnsey family home in Seneca Falls, N.Y.; letters, notes, clippings, and research materials about the Sullivan Expedition, historical markers, the Johnston Farm, the Montezuma Marsh bird refuge, Luke Swetland, Judge William I.Grubb, and other interests of Cyrus Garnsey Jr.; also a family history byElizabeth Pettis Lamb, 1984; and letters relating to the Iroquois turtle shell rattle from Chief Jesse Lyons, Chief of the Onondagas
Scope and content
Maps and letters relating to Cayuga Lake Park; early material relating to Cyrus Garnsey's attending Brown University and his obituaries; Croix de Guerre and memorial certificate for Cyrus Garnsey III, who died in World War I; and letters and pictures relating to Cyrus Garnsey, Jr. and his resemblance to Robert E. Lee.
Scope and content
Also, "An Addition to the Garnsey Family History," typescript by Elizabeth Lamb, 1993.
Diaries. Cayuga Lake Park. Seneca County (N.Y.) -- History. United States -- History -- Revolution, 1775-1783 -- Campaigns and battles. Families -- New York (State) -- Seneca Falls. Agriculture. Garnsey, Cyrus. Garnsey, Cyrus, Jr. Garnsey, Cyrus, III. Lamb, Elizabeth Pettis. Pettis, Mary Woolsey. Harris, Ira. Lyons, Jesse. Garnsey family. United States. Fuel Administration
2067717 <<<<<<< HEAD Diaries, 1850-1871
Scope and Contents
Each contain same accounts at the endJan. 1850-Jan. 18511Jan. 1851-Dec. 18521Jan. 1852-Jan. 18531Jan. 1853-Jan. 18541Jan. 1854-Mar. 18551Mar.-Nov. 19551Nov. 1855-Sept. 18561Aug. 1856-July 18571July 1857-May 18581May 1858-Sept. 18591Aug. 1859-June 18601July 1861-Oct. 18621Oct. 1862-Feb. 18641Mar. 1864-Dec. 18651Jan. 1866-Jan. 18681Jan. 1869-Jan. 18711Photocopies of diaries2Photocopies of diaries3Scrapbook of Cyrus Garnsey III put together by his father (Cyrus Garnsey Jr.) about Cyrus Garnsey III's participation in World War I4Letters to Cyrus (nephew of) and Mercy (sister of) Ira Harris1847-184951Letters to Cyrus and Mercy from Ira Harris185052Letters to Cyrus and Mercy from Ira Harris185153Letters to Cyrus and Mercy from Ira Harris185254Letters to Cyrus and Mercy from Ira Harris1853-185555Letters to Cyrus and Mercy from Ira Harris1856-185756Letters to Cyrus and Mercy from Ira Harris1861-187057Ira Harris Family Papers58[From Binder] Personal letters, clippings, and telegrams; Cyrus Garnsey Jr. - United States Fuel Administration1917591918 Jan.-Feb.5101918 May 3-45111918 May 5-85121918 May 9-145131918 May 16-29 (includes Woodrow Wilson letter)1918 May 16-295141918 June-Dec.5151919 Jan.-Dec.51619205171921-19275181937-1951519Skit by Washington Newspapermen520Typescript of diary1918-1919521Certificates and proclamations522Estate papers of Cyrus Garnsey, Jr.523-24Correspondence about Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s finances with Mary Woolsey Pettis525-26Correspondence about Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s finances with Mary Woolsey Pettis, also inventory of homestead, and clerk's certificate of the search of the premises of Homer A. Newell527Assessment of property of Cyrus Garnsey Jr.1853528Contract with Thomas Reynolds and Cyrus Garnsey1856529Letters, notes, clippings, and research on the Sullivan Expedition, commemoration and the placing of historical markers (by Cyrus Garnsey Jr.)61-5
Scope and Contents
Includes material from the Finger Lakes Association, Robert Treman, Cornell Indian Boards, mention of Erl Bates.Annotated map of old Garnsey homestead66Notes on historical markers67Clippings on historical markers68Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s papers about Johnston Farm69Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s Johnston farm blueprints610Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s collected articles about Johston farm611Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s papers about Montezuma Marsh bird refuge612Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s collected papers about Luke Swetland613Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s collected desk notes and small notebook dated "March 4, 1885 - Cleveland and Hendricks Inauguration"614Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s collected clippings and notes on lotteries and gambling615Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s collected notes about Judge William I. Grubb616Papers to Cyrus Garnsey Jr. from Sanford Morison about Alabama Mines1912617Misc. about career of Cyrus Garnsey Jr.618Cyrus Garnsey Jr.'s calendar619Misc. papers of Cyrus Garnsey Jr.620Family of Cyrus Garnsey Jr. Genealogy by Elizabeth Pettis Lamb1984621Reprint of the "Method of Fixing Prices of Bituminous Coal adopted by the United States Fuel Administration," by Cyrus Garnsey Jr., R.V. Norris, and J.H. Allport1918622Memorials of Ira Harris1876623A Genealogy of Nicholas Harris, M.D.1904624Sullivan's Campaign, A. Tiffany Norton1879625"A Logical State Park Project" (Red Jacket Memorial Park), and items by Alexander M. Stewart with signature626An Addition to the Garnsey Family History, typescript by Elizabeth Lamb1993627Oversized material1
Scope and Contents
Includes print of Robert E. Lee on horseback by David Bendann. Certificate for the Croix de Guerre given to Cyrus Garnsey. World War One memorial certificate. Annotated map of the Sullivan Expedition near Cayuga Lake. Map of the Senecal Villages and the Jesuit and French Contacts 1615-1708. ======= Diaries, 1850-1871
